blocks:
Sūrat Ar-Ra‘d

Medinan, and it is 43 verses

Sūrat Ar-Ra‘d

(It is entirely Makkan, and it is said: except for the last verse of it. And it is said: it is Medinan, except for two verses that were revealed in Mecca: وَلَوْ أَنَّ قُرْآناً سُيِّرَتْ بِهِ الْجِبالُ (13:31) and what follows it. And the number of its verses is forty-three verses.) بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) المر ** (Alif. Lām. Mīm. Rā.) The discussion concerning it and its counterparts has already preceded. In al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Its meaning is: I am Allah, the Reviver (المحيي), the Giver of Death (المُميتُ), the Provider (الرزاق). تِلْكَ آياتُ الْكِتابِ وَالَّذِي أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْكَ مِنْ رَبِّكَ الْحَقُّ وَلكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ (١) (These are the signs of the Book, and what has been sent down to you from your Lord is the truth, but most people do not believe.) —meaning the Quran—الْحَقُّ وَلكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ**.

(٢) اللهُ الَّذِي رَفَعَ السَّماواتِ بِغَيْرِ عَمَدٍ ** (2) Allah is He who raised the heavens without any pillars that you see, without supports (أساطين). تَرَوْنَها ** (that you see): This is an adjective (صفة) for عَمَدٍ. (pillars). Al-Qummi and al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): "There are pillars (عمد), but you do not see them." ثُمَّ اسْتَوى عَلَى الْعَرْشِ ** then settled on the Throne, Its meaning preceded in Surat al-A'raf. وَسَخَّرَ الشَّمْسَ وَالْقَمَرَ كُلٌّ يَجْرِي لِأَجَلٍ مُسَمًّى (and He subjected the sun and the moon, each running for a specified term): meaning, for a specific duration (لمدة معينة) in which its cycles are completed, or until a fixed limit (لغاية مضروبة) before which its course is cut off, and that is إِذَا الشَّمْسُ كُوِّرَتْ وَإِذَا النُّجُومُ انْكَدَرَتْ يُدَبِّرُ الْأَمْرَ ** (When the sun is wrapped up, and when the stars fall, He directs the command): meaning, the command of His dominion (ملكوت) concerning creation (الإيجاد), annihilation (الإعدام), giving life (الإحياء), causing death (الإِماتة), and other than that. يُفَصِّلُ الْآياتِ ** (elaborating the signs): He sends them down and clarifies them (يُنزلها ويبيّنها). لَعَلَّكُمْ بِلِقاءِ رَبِّكُمْ تُوقِنُونَ ** (so that you may be certain of the encounter with your Lord): so that you may reflect upon them and realize the perfection of His power and His craftsmanship (صنعه) in everything, and thus know that He encompasses everything. And this is like His saying: أَلا إِنَّهُمْ فِي مِرْيَةٍ مِنْ لِقاءِ رَبِّهِمْ أَلا إِنَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ مُحِيطٌ. (Indeed, they are in doubt about the encounter with their Lord. Indeed, He encompasses all things.)

footnote (١) Meaning by *al-Kitab* (*الكتاب*) is the *Surah* (*السّورة*), and **تِلْكَ ** (those) is a reference (*إشارة*) to its verses (*آياتها*), meaning: those verses are the complete verses of the *Surah*.

(٣) وَهُوَ الَّذِي مَدَّ الْأَرْضَ ** (3) And He is the One who spread out the earth He spread it out lengthwise and breadthwise so that feet may be firm upon it and animals may move about upon it. وَجَعَلَ فِيها رَواسِيَ ** and set in it firm mountains fixed mountains (ثوابت) (1) وَأَنْهاراً ** and rivers which are generated from them. وَمِنْ كُلِّ الثَّمَراتِ جَعَلَ فِيها زَوْجَيْنِ اثْنَيْنِ ** and made in it two pairs of every kind of fruit. And He made in it, from all its types, two categories (صنفين اثنين): black and white, and sweet and sour, moist and dry, small and large, and what resembles that from the differing categories (الأصناف المختلفة). يُغْشِي اللَّيْلَ النَّهارَ ** He draws the night's cover over the day. He clothes the darkness of the night over the brightness of the day, so the atmosphere becomes dark after it had been bright. إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآياتٍ لِقَوْمٍ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ (٢). There are indeed signs in that for a people who reflect.**

(٤) وَفِي الْأَرْضِ قِطَعٌ مُتَجاوِراتٌ ** (4) And in the earth are tracts neighbouring one another, contiguous, of good (fertile) and saline (سبخة) (3), and soft and hard, and suitable for cultivation (الزرع) but not for trees (الشجر), and vice versa, and not suitable for either of them. وَجَنَّاتٌ مِنْ أَعْنابٍ وَزَرْعٌ وَنَخِيلٌ ** and gardens of vines, and crops, and date-palms, In them are types of grapes (الأعناب), cultivation (الزرع), and date-palms (النخيل). And وَزَرْعٌ and وَنَخِيلٌ were read with al-rafʿ (الرفع) (nominative case), and likewise in what is conjoined to them. صِنْوانٌ ** some having common roots Date-palms whose origin (أصلها) is one. وَغَيْرُ صِنْوانٍ ** and others having separate roots, Separate (متفرقات), differing in origins (مختلفة الأصول), or similar (أمثال) and dissimilar (وغير أمثال). And in the Prophetic Hadith: "A man's paternal uncle (عمّ) is the ṣinw (صنو) of his father." يُسْقى ** And it was read with the yāʾ (بالياءِ). بِماءٍ واحِدٍ وَنُفَضِّلُ ** And it was read with the yāʾ (بالياءِ). بَعْضَها عَلى بَعْضٍ فِي الْأُكُلِ ** all irrigated with the same water, and We favour some of them over others in flavour. (4) In the fruit (الثمر): in form (شكلاً), quantity (قدراً), scent (رائحةً), and taste (طعماً).

Al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ) narrated from them (peace be upon them): He means that this good land (الأرض الطيبة) is neighboring this saline land (الأرض المالحة), but it is not part of it, just as a people (القوم) may neighbor another people (القوم) yet are not part of them.

And in al-Majmaʿ (المجمع), narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace), that He said to Ali (peace be upon him): "People are from various trees (شجر شتّى), but I and you are from one tree (شجرة واحدة)." Then He recited this Ayah.

إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآياتٍ لِقَوْمٍ يَعْقِلُونَ ** There are indeed signs in that for a people who apply reason. They use their intellects (عقولهم) for contemplation (بالتفكر), whereby they are guided to the greatness of the Maker (الصانع), His knowledge (علمه), His profound wisdom (حكمته البالغة), His penetrating power (قدرته النافذة), His perfect management (تدبيره الكامل), His comprehensive kindness (لطفه الشامل), and the excellence of His nurturing (حسن تربيته) and His creations (وصنايعه) little by little (شيئاً فشيئاً) until they reach the ultimate limit of the perfections (منتهى كمالاتها) appropriate for them.

(5) وَإِنْ تَعْجَبْ ** O Muhammad (يا محمّد), at their saying regarding the denial of the Resurrection (إنكار البعث), فَعَجَبٌ قَوْلُهُمْ ** (their saying is indeed amazing). It is truly worthy of astonishment (فحقيق بأن يتعجب منه), for whoever was able to originate what He has recounted to you, the repetition (recreation) would be easier for Him (كانت الإعادة أهون عليه). أَإِذا كُنَّا تُراباً أَإِنَّا لَفِي خَلْقٍ جَدِيدٍ (1) (If you are amazed, their saying is indeed amazing: 'When we have become dust, shall we really be in a new creation?')

أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِرَبِّهِمْ ** (They are those who disbelieve in their Lord) because of their denial of His power (لانكارهم قدرته) and their persistence in disbelief (وتماديهم في الكفر). (2)

وَأُولئِكَ الْأَغْلالُ فِي أَعْناقِهِمْ ** (and they are those who will have shackles on their necks). They are bound by misguidance (مقيدون بالضلال); their deliverance is not hoped for due to their persistence (لإصرارهم). أُولئِكَ أَصْحابُ النَّارِ هُمْ فِيها خالِدُونَ ** (and they will be the inmates of the Fire, and they will remain in it forever). They will not be separated from it (لا ينفكون عنها).

(٦) وَيَسْتَعْجِلُونَكَ بِالسَّيِّئَةِ قَبْلَ الْحَسَنَةِ ** (6) They ask you to hasten the evil before the good. (Meaning) with the punishment (بالعقوبة) before the well-being (قبل العافية). That is because they hastened the torment (بالعذاب) mockingly. وَقَدْ خَلَتْ ** though there have passed (Meaning) passed. مِنْ قَبْلِهِمُ الْمَثُلاتُ ** before them exemplary punishments. (Meaning) the punishments (عقوبات) of their likes among the deniers. So what is wrong with them that they do not take heed from them?

In Nahj al-Balaghah: Beware of what befell the nations before you—the exemplary punishments (المثلاث) due to evil deeds (بسوءِ الأفعال) and blameworthy actions (وذميم الأعمال). So remember their circumstances in good and evil, and beware of becoming like them.

وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَذُو مَغْفِرَةٍ لِلنَّاسِ عَلى ظُلْمِهِمْ ** Indeed your Lord is forgiving to mankind despite their wrongdoing, meaning, despite their wronging themselves with sins (بالذنُوب). وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَشَدِيدُ الْعِقابِ.** and indeed your Lord is severe in retribution.

In al-Majma': When this Ayah was revealed, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Were it not for the pardon (عفو) and forbearance (تجاوزه) of Allah, no one would enjoy life, and were it not for the threat (وعيد) and punishment (عقابه) of Allah, everyone would become complacent (لاتّكل)."

And in al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Rida عليه السلام: When they discussed the major sins (الكبائر) and the saying of the Mu'tazilah (المعتزلة) concerning them—that they are not forgiven—Abu 'Abdillah عليه السلام said: "The Quran has been revealed contrary to the saying of the Mu'tazilah (المعتزلة)." He, majestic is His glory, said: وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَذُو مَغْفِرَةٍ لِلنَّاسِ عَلى ظُلْمِهِمْ. (Indeed your Lord is forgiving to mankind despite their wrongdoing).

(٧) وَيَقُولُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَوْ لا أُنْزِلَ عَلَيْهِ آيَةٌ مِنْ رَبِّهِ (7) The faithless say, 'Why has not a sign been sent down to him from his Lord?' They did not acknowledge the signs that were sent down, out of obstinacy (عناداً), and they proposed something similar to what Moses and Jesus were given. إِنَّما أَنْتَ مُنْذِرٌ (7) You are only a warner, [You are] sent for warning (للإنذار), like others among the Messengers, and incumbent upon you is only the bringing of that by which it is confirmed that you are a Messenger, a fearful warner (مُنْذِر). And all the signs are equal in achieving the purpose. وَلِكُلِّ قَوْمٍ هادٍ (7) and there is a guide for every people. [A guide] who guides them to the religion and calls them to Allah through a form of guidance (الهداية) and by a sign with which He has distinguished him.

In al-Majma': When this verse was revealed, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "I am the warner (المنذر) and Ali is the guide (الهادي) after me. O Ali, through you the guided ones (المهتدون) are guided."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: "The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم is the warner (المنذر), and for every time there is a guide (هاد) from us who guides them to what the Prophet of Allah brought. Then the guides (الهداة) after him are Ali, then the successors (الأوصياء), one after the other."

And narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: "Every Imam is a guide (هاد) for the generation (القرن) in which he exists." And the like of it is found in al-Ikmal, and it was narrated by al-Qummi, al-Ayyashi, and more than one person from the elite (الخاصّة) and the general public (العامّة) through more than one chain of transmission (الأسانيد).

And [the narration by] al-Qummi is a refutation against those who deny that there is an Imam in every age and time, and that the earth is never devoid of a proof (حجّة).

footnote (١) [This is] a substitute (*بدل*) for their saying, or an object for the sake of which (*مفعول له*), and the agent (*الفاعل*) in *إذا* is omitted, indicated by **لَفِي خَلْقٍ جَدِيدٍ** (are indeed in a new creation).

footnote (٢) *تمادى في الذّنوب* (He persisted in sins): He insisted (*لجّ*), continued, and expanded upon them. Similar examples are *تمادى في الجهل* (He persisted in ignorance) and *تمادى في غيّه* (He persisted in his error) (M.).

(8) اللهُ يَعْلَمُ ما تَحْمِلُ كُلُّ أُنْثى ** (8) Allah knows what every female carries, (It means) whether male or female, complete or deficient, beautiful or ugly, blessed (سعيد) or wretched (شقي). وَما تَغِيضُ (1) الْأَرْحامُ and what the wombs diminish, (meaning) the wombs, and what they decrease. وَما تَزْدادُ ** and what they augment. in duration, number, and formation (الخلقة).

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from one of the two Imams (peace be upon them): Al-Ghayd (الغيض) is every pregnancy less than nine months, and what is augmented (ما تزداد) is everything that increases beyond nine months. Whenever a woman sees menstrual blood (الدم من الحيض) during her pregnancy, she is augmented by the number of days she saw blood during her pregnancy.

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): ما تَحْمِلُ كُلُّ أُنْثى ** (what every female carries) means the male and the female. وَما تَغِيضُ الْأَرْحامُ ** (and what the wombs diminish) is what is less than nine (months), and that is ghayd (غيض). وَما تَزْدادُ (and what they augment) is what she sees of blood during her pregnancy; by this, it is augmented beyond nine months. And in a narration: ما تَغِيضُ ** (what they diminish) is what was not a pregnancy. وَما تَزْدادُ ** (and what they augment) is the male and the female combined.

And in al-Qummi: وَما تَغِيضُ ** (what they diminish) is what is miscarried before completion. وَما تَزْدادُ ** (and what they augment) is beyond nine months; whenever the woman sees menstruation (حيض) during the days of her pregnancy, that increases her pregnancy. وَكُلُّ شَيْءٍ عِنْدَهُ بِمِقْدارٍ ** and everything is with Him by [a precise] measure. (meaning) by a measure (بقدر) which it neither exceeds nor falls short of.

(1) The water diminished (غاض الماء يغيض غيضاً) from the category (باب) of sāra (سار) and mughāḍan (مغاضاً), meaning it decreased and dried up in the earth. Inghāḍa (انغاض) is similar. Ghīḍa al-māʾ (غيض الماء) means that was done to it. His saying: وَما تَغِيضُ الْأَرْحامُ ** (and what the wombs diminish) means they decrease below the duration of pregnancy with which the child survives (م).

(9) عالِمُ الْغَيْبِ وَالشَّهادَةِ الْكَبِيرُ الْمُتَعالِ (The Knower of the Unseen and the Seen, the Great, the Exalted.) عالِمُ الْغَيْبِ ** is that which the sense does not perceive, وَالشَّهادَةِ ** is that which it perceives (1). الْكَبِيرُ ** is the One of magnificent status, beside Whom everything is insignificant, الْمُتَعالِ ** is the One Who is elevated above everything by His greatness. (10) سَواءٌ مِنْكُمْ مَنْ أَسَرَّ الْقَوْلَ وَمَنْ جَهَرَ بِهِ وَمَنْ هُوَ مُسْتَخْفٍ بِاللَّيْلِ وَسارِبٌ بِالنَّهارِ (It is the same [to Him] whether any of you conceals his speech or discloses it, and whether he hides himself in the night or moves about in the day.) سَواءٌ مِنْكُمْ مَنْ أَسَرَّ الْقَوْلَ ** within himself, وَمَنْ جَهَرَ بِهِ ** to others, وَمَنْ هُوَ مُسْتَخْفٍ بِاللَّيْلِ (and whoever is hiding himself by night) meaning: seeking concealment in a hiding place (2) by night, وَسارِبٌ (3) meaning: prominent/visible by day, whom everyone sees. Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He means that the secret (السّرّ) and the public (العلانية) are the same to Him.

(١١) لَهُ ** (He has) for the one who conceals (أسرَّ) or reveals (جهر) or hides (استخفى) or goes forth (سرب) مُعَقِّباتٌ ** (guardian angels, succeeding one another) angels who succeed one another in guarding him and protecting him (وكلاءته) مِنْ بَيْنِ يَدَيْهِ وَمِنْ خَلْفِهِ ** (before him and behind him) from his sides (جوانبه). يَحْفَظُونَهُ مِنْ أَمْرِ اللهِ ** (who guard him by Allah’s command.) It was said: [It means] because of Allah's command (من اجل أمر اللهِ), meaning: because Allah commanded them to guard him.

And in al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), that this Ayah was recited in His presence. He said to the reciter: "Are you not Arabs (عَرْباء)? How can the succeeding angels (المعقبات) be from before him, when the succeeding angel (المعقب) is only from behind him?" The man said: "May I be made a sacrifice for you, how is this?" He (peace be upon him) said: "It was only revealed as: لَهُ مُعَقِّباتٌ مِنْ خَلْفِهِ ورقيب مِنْ بَيْنِ يَدَيْهِ يَحْفَظُونَهُ بأمر اللهِ (He has succeeding angels from behind him, and a watcher (رقيب) from before him, who guard him by Allah’s command). And who is it that can guard a thing from the command of Allah (أمر اللهِ)? They are the angels entrusted (الموكّلُونَ) with mankind." Al-Ayyashi narrated a similar account from him (peace be upon him).

And in al-Manaqib and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): مِنْ أَمْرِ اللهِ ** (by Allah’s command) He says: By Allah's command, [they guard him] from falling into a well (ركيّ) (4) or a wall falling upon him, or anything afflicting him, until the Divine Decree (القدر) arrives. Then they leave him alone with it, handing him over to the decrees (المقادير). They are two angels who guard him by night and two angels by day, succeeding one another.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) what is close to this. And in al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): They are angels who guard him from destruction (المهالك) until they bring him to the decrees (المقادير), at which point they leave him alone with the decrees. إِنَّ اللهَ لا يُغَيِّرُ ما بِقَوْمٍ ** (Indeed, Allah does not change the state of a people) of well-being (العافية) and blessing (النِّعمة) حَتَّى يُغَيِّرُوا ما بِأَنْفُسِهِمْ ** (until they change what is in their own souls) from beautiful states (الأحوال الجملية) to ugly states (الأحوال القبيحَة).

Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah has decreed a binding decree (قضاءً حتماً): He does not bestow a blessing upon His servant and then strip it away from him before the servant commits a sin by which he deserves the stripping away of that blessing. That is the saying of Allah: إِنَّ اللهَ لا يُغَيِّرُ ما بِقَوْمٍ حَتَّى يُغَيِّرُوا ما بِأَنْفُسِهِمْ (Indeed, Allah does not change the state of a people until they change what is in their own souls.)

And in al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): The sins that change blessings are oppression (البغي) against people, abandoning the habit of doing good (الزوال عن العادة في الخير) and performing kindness (اصطناع المعروف), ingratitude for blessings (كفران النعم), and abandoning gratitude (ترك الشكر). Then He recited the Ayah: وَإِذا أَرادَ اللهُ بِقَوْمٍ سُوْءاً فَلا مَرَدَّ لَهُ وَما لَهُمْ مِنْ دُونِهِ مِنْ والٍ (And when Allah wills to inflict a wrong on a people, there is no turning it back, and they have no protector besides Him.) —meaning, one who manages their affair (يلي أمرهم) and repels the evil (السُّوء) from them.

(١٢) هُوَ الَّذِي يُرِيكُمُ الْبَرْقَ خَوْفاً ** (12) He is the One who shows you the lightning, fear) from its harm, **وَطَمَعاً ** (and hope) in the rain.

In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): خَوْفاً ** (fear) is for the traveler, and وَطَمَعاً ** (and hope) is for the resident.

وَيُنْشِئُ السَّحابَ الثِّقالَ (and He raises the heavy clouds) Al-Qummi (القمّيّ): meaning He raises them from the earth.

هُوَ الَّذِي يُرِيكُمُ الْبَرْقَ خَوْفاً وَطَمَعاً وَيُنْشِئُ السَّحابَ الثِّقالَ (12) He is the One who shows you the lightning, fear and hope, and raises the heavy clouds.

(13) وَيُسَبِّحُ الرَّعْدُ بِحَمْدِهِ (13) The thunder glorifies Him with praise

It is narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم that he was asked about الرَّعْدُ ** (the thunder), and he said: "An angel entrusted with the clouds, having with him fiery scourges (مخاريق من نار) with which he drives the clouds."

And in al-Faqih, it is narrated: That الرَّعْدُ ** (the thunder) is the sound of an angel, larger than a fly (الذباب) and smaller than a wasp (الزّنبور).

And in it (al-Faqih) and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): That it is like the position of a man who is among the camels and drives them away, saying hāy hāy (هاي هاي), similar to that manner.

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: When he heard the sound of thunder, he would say: "Glory be to Him Whom the thunder glorifies with His praise **وَالْمَلائِكَةُ مِنْ خِيفَتِهِ ** (and the angels for awe of Him)."

(meaning) out of fear (خوفه) and reverence (إجلاله) for Him. وَيُرْسِلُ (And He sends) الصَّواعِقَ فَيُصِيبُ بِها مَنْ يَشاءُ ** (the thunderbolts, striking with them whomever He wishes), meaning: He destroys him. وَهُمْ يُجادِلُونَ فِي اللهِ ** (while they dispute concerning Allah), meaning: where they deny the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم regarding what he describes of His uniqueness (التفرّد) in divinity (بالألوهية), the resurrection of people (إعادة الناس), and their recompense (مجازاتهم). وَهُوَ شَدِيدُ الْمِحالِ ** (and He is severe in might), meaning: the plotting (المماحلة) and scheming (المكايدة) against His enemies. And it is said (it is derived) from al-maḥl (المحلّ) meaning strength (القوّة).

And al-Qummi (said): meaning severe in wrath (شديد الغضب). And in al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): severe in seizing (شديد الأخذ).

And in al-Amali: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم sent a man to a Pharaoh (فرعون) among the Pharaohs of the Arabs, inviting him to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic. He said to the messenger: "Inform me about the One to Whom you invite me: Is He made of silver (فضّة), or of gold (ذهب), or of iron (حديد)?" So he returned to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and informed him of his saying. The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Return to him and invite him." He said: "O Prophet of Allah, he is more rebellious (أعتى) than that." He said: "Return to him." So he returned to him, and he spoke the same words as before. While he was speaking to him, a cloud thundered a thunderclap and cast a thunderbolt (صاعقة) upon his head, which went to split his skull (يقحف رأسه). Then Allah, majestic is His praise, revealed: يُرْسِلُ الصَّواعِقَ (He sends the thunderbolts) [the rest of] the Ayah.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): That الصَّواعِقَ ** (the thunderbolts) do not strike one who remembers (Allah) (ذاكِراً). It was asked: "And who is the one who remembers (الذاكر)?" He said: "One who recites one hundred Ayahs (مائة آية)."

(14) لَهُ دَعْوَةُ الْحَقِ (To Him belongs the call of the truth.) For He is called upon, and He responds. وَالَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ (and those whom they invoke) whom the polytheists invoke مِنْ دُونِهِ لا يَسْتَجِيبُونَ لَهُمْ بِشَيْءٍ (besides Him do not answer them in any wise) from the requests (الطلبات) إِلَّا كَباسِطِ كَفَّيْهِ (except as one who stretches out his palms) except for a response like the response of one who stretches out his palms إِلَى الْماءِ لِيَبْلُغَ فاهُ (towards the water so that it may reach his mouth) asking it to reach him from afar, or scooping it up while stretching out his palms to drink it. وَما هُوَ بِبالِغِهِ (but it does not reach it.) because water is inanimate (جماد); it neither perceives his invocation nor is it able to respond to him, nor does it settle in the outstretched palm. And likewise are their deities (آلهتهم).

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): This is a parable set forth by Allah for those who worship idols (الأصنام) and those who worship deities (الآلهة) besides Allah. So لا يَسْتَجِيبُونَ لَهُمْ بِشَيْءٍ إِلَّا كَباسِطِ كَفَّيْهِ إِلَى الْماءِ (do not answer them in any wise except as one who stretches out his palms towards the water) seeking to take it from afar, but it does not reach him. وَما دُعاءُ الْكافِرِينَ إِلَّا فِي ضَلالٍ (The invocation of the faithless is nothing but error.) in loss (ضياع) and futility (بطلان).

(١٥) وَلِلَّهِ يَسْجُدُ مَنْ فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ طَوْعاً وَكَرْهاً وَظِلالُهُمْ بِالْغُدُوِّ وَالْآصالِ ** (15) And to Allah prostrates whoever is in the heavens and the earth, willingly or unwillingly, and their shadows, in the mornings and the evenings. (The word الْآصالِ means the evenings (العشيِ).)

In al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): As for those among the inhabitants of the heavens who prostrate willingly (طوعاً), they are the angels who prostrate to Allah willingly. And as for those among the inhabitants of the earth who prostrate, whoever was born into Islam prostrates to Him willingly. And as for those who prostrate to Him unwillingly (كرهاً), they are those who were compelled (جُبِرَ) into Islam. And as for those who do not prostrate, their shadow prostrates in the morning (بالغداة) and the evening (العشيّ).

And al-Qummi said: The shifting (تحويل) of every shadow that Allah created is a prostration (سجود) to Allah, because there is nothing except that it has a shadow which moves by its movement, and its shifting is its prostration. He mentioned this in Surat al-Nahl. And it was said that what is meant by the shadow (الظل) is the body (الجَسَد), and that the body is called the shadow because the shadow originates from it, and because it is a shadow for the soul (الروح)—since the body is dark (ظلماني) and the soul is luminous (نوراني)—and it is subservient to the soul, moving by its psychic movement (حَرَكتِه النفسانية) and resting by its psychic rest (سكونه النفساني).

Al-Qummi said: The shadow of the believer prostrates willingly (طوعاً), and the shadow of the disbeliever prostrates unwillingly (كرهاً). This refers to their growth (نموّهم), their movement (حركتهم), their increase (زيادتهم), and their decrease (نقصانهم).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding His saying: وَظِلالُهُمْ بِالْغُدُوِّ وَالْآصالِ ** (and their shadows, in the mornings and the evenings). He said: It is the supplication (الدعاء) before the rising of the sun and before its setting, and that is an hour of response (ساعة إجابة).

And in Nahj al-Balagha: Blessed is He to Whom يَسْجُدُ ** (prostrates) مَنْ فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ طَوْعاً وَكَرْهاً ** (whoever is in the heavens and the earth, willingly or unwillingly). And for Him, one rubs the cheek (خدّاً) and the face (وجهاً) in the dust, and submits to Him in obedience (الطاعة) with peace (سلماً) and weakness (ضعفاً), and yields the reins (القِياد) (1) out of awe (رهبة) and fear (خوفاً). He (the narrator/Imam) said: And the trees (الأشجار) prostrated to Him in the mornings (بالغُدُوّ) and the evenings (الآصال).

I say: Just as it is permissible that the known meaning of prostration (السجُود), shadow (الظل), morning (الغُدو), and evening (الآصال) is intended for each of them, so too is it permissible that prostration (السجود) means submission (الانقياد), shadow (الظل) means the body (الجسد), and morning and evening (الغدّو والآصال) mean permanence (الدوام). It is also permissible that what is intended by each of them is that which encompasses both meanings, so that it applies to every thing according to its nature and what is appropriate for it. By this approach, the narratives (الروايات) and the sayings (الأقوال) are reconciled. A further explanation of this meaning will come in Surat al-Nahl, if Allah wills (إن شاء الله).

(١٦) قُلْ مَنْ رَبُّ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** (16) Say, ‘Who is the Lord of the heavens and the earth?’ (Who is) their Creator and the Manager of their affairs (أمرهما / أمورهما - variant reading). قُلِ اللهُ ** Say, ‘Allah.’ Respond to them with that, since they have no answer other than Him, and because He is the manifest One about Whom there is no doubt. قُلْ أَفَاتَّخَذْتُمْ مِنْ دُونِهِ أَوْلِياءَ ** Say, ‘Have you then taken guardians besides Him, Then He obligates them with that (answer). لا يَمْلِكُونَ لِأَنْفُسِهِمْ نَفْعاً وَلا ضَرًّا ** who have no power to benefit or harm themselves?’ So how (can they benefit or harm) others? قُلْ هَلْ يَسْتَوِي الْأَعْمى وَالْبَصِيرُ. Say, ‘Are the blind and the seeing equal? Al-Qummi said: Meaning the disbeliever (الكافر) and the believer (المؤمن). أَمْ هَلْ تَسْتَوِي الظُّلُماتُ وَالنُّورُ ** Or are the darknesses and the light equal?’ He said: (Meaning) disbelief (الكفر) and faith (الايمان). And it was read (يستوي) with the yāʾ (بالياءِ). أَمْ جَعَلُوا لِلَّهِ شُرَكاءَ ** Have they appointed partners to Allah Rather, (it means) أَجَعَلُوا (Have they appointed?), and the hamzah (الهمزة) is for denial/reproach (للإنكار). خَلَقُوا كَخَلْقِهِ who created like His creation, (This phrase) is an attribute (صفة) for the partners (شركاء) included in the scope of the denial (الإنكار). فَتَشابَهَ الْخَلْقُ عَلَيْهِمْ ** so that the creation seemed alike to them? (Meaning) the creation of Allah and their creation. The meaning is that they did not take partners for Allah who were creators like Him, such that the creation would seem alike to them, leading them to say: "These (partners) created as Allah created, so they deserve worship just as He deserves it." Rather, they took partners who are incapable (عاجزين), unable to do what creatures (الخلق) are able to do, let alone what the Creator (الخالق) is able to do. قُلِ اللهُ خالِقُ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ Say, ‘Allah is the Creator of all things, There is no creator other than Him, so that He might be partnered with in worship. وَهُوَ الْواحِدُ الْقَهَّارُ and He is the One, the All-dominant.’ The One Who is unique in divinity (بالألوهيّة), the Dominant over all things.

(1). So-and-so is سلس قياد (smoothly led), meaning easy to lead (سهل الانقياد) without hesitation (من غير توقّف).

(١٧) أَنْزَلَ مِنَ السَّماءِ ماءً فَسالَتْ أَوْدِيَةٌ بِقَدَرِها ** (17) He sends down water from the sky, and the valleys flow according to their capacity. in smallness and largeness, and according to the expediency (المصلحة). فَاحْتَمَلَ السَّيْلُ زَبَداً رابِياً high/elevated. وَمِمَّا يُوقِدُونَ عَلَيْهِ فِي النَّارِ from the types of metals (الفلزّات) like gold, silver, iron, and copper. And it was read (توقدون) with the tāʾ (التاءِ). ابْتِغاءَ حِلْيَةٍ ** seeking ornament (حلية). أَوْ مَتاعٍ ** such as vessels (الأواني) and tools for cultivation (الحرث) and war (الحَرْب). زَبَدٌ مِثْلُهُ ** meaning, and from that which they ignite upon, there is a foam (زبد) like the foam of the water; it is its dross (خبثه). كَذلِكَ يَضْرِبُ اللهُ الْحَقَّ وَالْباطِلَ ** (17) Thus does Allah draw comparisons between the truth and falsehood. meaning, the likeness of the two (truth and falsehood) is like the truth in its benefit and permanence, likened to the water that descends from the sky, causing the valleys to flow according to need (الحاجة) and expediency (المصْلَحَةِ), so that various kinds of benefits are derived from it, and it remains in the earth by some of it settling in its sources (منابعه) and some of it traveling through the veins of the earth to the springs (العيون) and wells (الآبار); and likened to the metal (الفلزّ) from which benefit is derived by forming ornaments (الحليّ) and making various goods (الأمتعة), and this lasts for a long period. And falsehood is likened in its lack of benefit and speed of vanishing to their foam (زبدهما). فَأَمَّا الزَّبَدُ فَيَذْهَبُ جُفاءً (17) As for the foam, it vanishes as scum. it is cast off (يجفأ به), meaning, the torrent (السّيل) or the molten metal (الفلزّ المذابُ) throws it away. وَأَمَّا ما يَنْفَعُ النَّاسَ like the water and the essence of the metal (خلاصة الفلزّ), فَيَمْكُثُ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** it remains in the earth. its inhabitants benefit from it. كَذلِكَ يَضْرِبُ اللهُ الْأَمْثالَ Thus does Allah draw the parables (الْأَمْثالَ) to clarify ambiguous matters (المشتبهات).

Al-Qummi says: He sent down the Truth (الحقّ) from the sky, and the hearts (القلوب) carried it according to their desires (أهوائها): the one possessing certainty (ذو اليقين) according to the measure of his certainty, and the one possessing doubt (ذو الشكّ) according to the measure of his doubt. Thus, desire (الهوى) carried much falsehood (باطلاً) or scum (جفاءً). So, the water (الماء) is the Truth (الحق), the valleys (الأودية) are the hearts (القلوب), the torrent (السيل) is the desire (الهوى), and the foam (الزبد) and the dross of the ornament (خبث الحلية) is falsehood (الباطل), while the ornament (الحلية) and the goods (المتاع) are the Truth (الحق). Whoever attains the ornament (الحلية) and the goods (المتاع) in this world benefits from them, and likewise, the possessor of the Truth (صاحب الحق) on the Day of Resurrection will benefit from it. And whoever attains the foam (الزبد) and the dross of the ornament (خبث الحلية) in this world does not benefit from them, and likewise, the possessor of falsehood (صاحب الباطل) on the Day of Resurrection will not benefit from it.

And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Allah has clarified the stories of those who altered (the religion) (المغيّرين), and He drew their likeness in His saying: فَأَمَّا الزَّبَدُ فَيَذْهَبُ جُفاءً وَأَمَّا ما يَنْفَعُ النَّاسَ فَيَمْكُثُ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (17) As for the foam, it vanishes as scum. But as for that which benefits the people, it remains in the earth. So, the foam (الزبَدُ) in this context is the speech of the atheists (الملحدين) who established it in the Quran, and it will dissipate, become void, and vanish upon verification (التحصيل). And that which benefits the people from it is the true Revelation (التنزيل الحقيقي) which لا يَأْتِيهِ الْباطِلُ مِنْ بَيْنِ يَدَيْهِ وَلا مِنْ خَلْفِهِ ** (42) falsehood cannot approach from before it nor from behind it. And the hearts (القلوب) accept it, and the earth (الأرض) in this context is the place of knowledge (محلّ العلم) and its permanence (قراره). The narration (الحديث)—its entirety has already passed in the Sixth Introduction (المقدّمة السادسة).

(١٨) لِلَّذِينَ اسْتَجابُوا لِرَبِّهِمُ الْحُسْنى ** (18) For those who respond to their Lord is the best reward. The excellent response (الاستجابة الحسنى). وَالَّذِينَ لَمْ يَسْتَجِيبُوا لَهُ ** And those who do not respond to Him— Meaning: Thus does Allah strike the parables for the two groups. What follows is a new statement (كلام مبتدأ) intended to clarify the ultimate outcome (مآل) of those who did not respond. It is also possible that it is not connected to what precedes it, and that what is meant by al-husna (الْحُسْنى) is the excellent reward (المثوبة الحسنى), and that what follows is connected to it. لَوْ أَنَّ لَهُمْ ما فِي الْأَرْضِ جَمِيعاً وَمِثْلَهُ مَعَهُ لَافْتَدَوْا بِهِ أُولئِكَ لَهُمْ سُوءُ الْحِسابِ. if they owned all that is on the earth and as much of it besides, they would surely offer it as a ransom. For such there will be a terrible reckoning. In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It is that no good deed (حسنة) is accepted from them, nor is any evil deed (سيّئة) forgiven for them. And in the Hadith: Whoever is scrutinized (نوقش) in the reckoning (الحِسابِ) will be tormented (عذب). وَمَأْواهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ وَبِئْسَ الْمِهادُ ** and their refuge is hell, and it is an evil resting place. The abode (المستقر). Al-Qummi: They make beds (يمهدون) in the Fire.

(١٩) أَفَمَنْ يَعْلَمُ أَنَّما أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْكَ مِنْ رَبِّكَ الْحَقُ ** (19) Is he who knows that what has been sent down to you from your Lord is the truth فيستجيبُ كَمَنْ هُوَ أَعْمى ** so he responds like him who is blind? القلب لا يستبصر فيستجيب والهمزة للإنكار يعني لا شبهة في عدم تشابههما بعد ما ضرب من المثل فانّ بينهما من البون ما بين الزّبد والماءِ والخبث والأبريز of heart, who does not perceive clearly, so he does not respond? And the hamzah is for denial (الإنكار), meaning there is no doubt regarding the lack of similarity between the two after the parable that was struck, for indeed, the difference (البون) between them is like the difference between foam (الزّبد) and water (الماءِ), and dross (الخبث) and pure gold (الأبريز). إِنَّما يَتَذَكَّرُ أُولُوا الْأَلْبابِ ** Only those who possess intellect take admonition. ذوُو العقول المبرّأة عن مشايعة الالِفِ ومعارضة الوهم. Those who possess intellects (العقول) that are purified from following habit (مشايعة الالِفِ) and opposing illusion (معارضة الوهم). العيّاشيّ عن الصادق عليه السلام : أنّه خاطب شيعته بقوله أنتم أُولوا الألباب في كتاب اللهِ قال الله إِنَّما يَتَذَكَّرُ أُولُوا الْأَلْبابِ. Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that He addressed His followers with His saying: "You are أُولوا الْأَلْبابِ (those who possess intellect) in the Book of Allah." Allah said: إِنَّما يَتَذَكَّرُ أُولُوا الْأَلْبابِ.** (Only those who possess intellect take admonition.)

(٢٠) الَّذِينَ يُوفُونَ بِعَهْدِ اللهِ (Those who fulfill the covenant of Allah) what they have bound upon themselves for Allah. وَلا يَنْقُضُونَ الْمِيثاقَ (and do not break the compact) what they have confirmed of covenants (المواثيق) between them and Allah and between the servants (العباد). And this is a generalization after a specification (تعميم بعد التخصيص).

Al-Qummi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): This verse was revealed concerning the Household of Muhammad (peace be upon them), and what He covenanted with them, and what He took from them of the compact (الميثاق) in the primordial world (الذّر) regarding the wilayah (ولاية) of the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين) and the Imams after him (peace be upon them).

(21) وَالَّذِينَ يَصِلُونَ ما أَمَرَ اللهُ بِهِ أَنْ يُوصَلَ ** (21) And those who join what Allah has commanded to be joined from kinship (الرحم), especially the kinship of the Household of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and included within this is the loyalty (موالاة) to the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين) and the observance of their rights.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It was revealed concerning the kinship (رحم) of the Household of Muhammad, and it may also be concerning your relatives. Then He said: "So do not be among those who say that a thing is only in one thing."

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Kinship (الرّحم) is suspended from the Throne (العرش), saying: 'O Allah, join him who joins me, and cut off him who cuts me off.' And this is the kinship (رحم) of the Household of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and it is the saying of Allah: وَالَّذِينَ يَصِلُونَ ما أَمَرَ اللهُ بِهِ أَنْ يُوصَلَ (And those who join what Allah has commanded to be joined), and the kinship (رحم) of every possessor of kinship.

And in al-Ayyashi: And the kinship (رحم) of every believer.

And in al-Majma', al-Qummi, and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Kazim (peace be upon him): The like of it.

And in al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): And among what Allah has made obligatory concerning wealth, besides al-Zakat, is the Almighty's saying: الَّذِينَ يَصِلُونَ ما أَمَرَ اللهُ بِهِ أَنْ يُوصَلَ. (those who join what Allah has commanded to be joined.)

And in al-Majma': The like of it, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him). وَيَخْشَوْنَ رَبَّهُمْ وَيَخافُونَ سُوءَ الْحِسابِ ** (21) and fear their Lord, and dread the evil reckoning** specifically, so they reckon themselves before they are reckoned.

In al-Kafi, al-Ayyashi, al-Ma'ani, and al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that He recited this verse when He met a man who had demanded his full right (استقصى حقه) from his brother. And He said: "Do you think they fear that He will wrong them or oppress them? No. Rather, they feared the demanding of the full account (الاستقصاء) and the strict scrutiny (المداقة). So Allah named it the evil reckoning (سوء الحساب). Thus, whoever demands the full account (استقصى) has done evil (أساءَ)."

And in al-Majma' and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): That the evil deeds (السيئات) are counted against them and the good deeds (الحَسنات) are counted for them, and that is the demanding of the full account (الاستقصاء).

And in Misbah al-Shari'ah, narrated from him (peace be upon him): If there were no terror (مهولة) for the reckoning except the shame (حياء) of being presented before Allah and the disgrace (فضيحة) of the tearing of the veil (هتك السّتر) over hidden matters (المخفيّات), it would be right for a person not to descend from the peaks of the mountains, nor seek refuge in civilization (عمران), nor eat, nor drink, nor sleep, except out of necessity connected to perishing (التلف).

(٢٢) وَالَّذِينَ صَبَرُوا (22) And those who are patient in fulfilling the commands of Allah, enduring the hardships of religious obligations (التكاليف), bearing calamities affecting their souls (النفوس) and wealth (الأموال), and refraining from the disobedience (معاصِي) of Allah. ابْتِغاءَ وَجْهِ رَبِّهِمْ (seeking the Face of their Lord) seeking His pleasure. وَأَقامُوا الصَّلاةَ وَأَنْفَقُوا مِمَّا رَزَقْناهُمْ سِرًّا وَعَلانِيَةً وَيَدْرَؤُنَ بِالْحَسَنَةِ السَّيِّئَةَ (and maintain the prayer, and spend out of what We have provided them, secretly and openly, and repel evil with good) they repel it with it, thus recompensing evil (الإساءة) with goodness (الإحسان), and they follow up the evil deed (السيئة) with a good deed (الحَسَنة) so that it erases it.

Al-Qummi narrates from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said to Ali (peace be upon him): "O Ali, there is no house that experiences joy (فرحة) except that it is followed by sorrow (ترحة or نوحة - variant reading خ ل), and there is no worry (همّ) except that it has relief (فرج), except for the worry of the people of the Fire. If you commit an evil deed (سيّئة), follow it quickly with a good deed (حَسَنة) that erases it. And adhere to acts of goodness (صنائع الخير), for they repel the causes of evil (مصاريع السوءِ)." The Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) only spoke to the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in the manner of instructing (تأديب) the people, not because the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) had committed evil deeds (سيّئات). أُولئِكَ لَهُمْ عُقْبَى الدَّارِ (For such there is the ultimate outcome of the [eternal] abode) the consequence of the abode (عاقبة الدار) and what ought to be the final destination (مآل) of its inhabitants, which is Paradise (الجنّة).

(٢٣) جَنَّاتُ عَدْنٍ يَدْخُلُونَها ** (Gardens of Eden, which they will enter) Al-ʿAdn (العدن) means permanence (الإقامة), meaning: Gardens in which they will reside permanently. Narrations concerning them have already passed in Surat al-Tawbah. وَمَنْ صَلَحَ مِنْ آبائِهِمْ وَأَزْواجِهِمْ وَذُرِّيَّاتِهِمْ ** (and those who are righteous among their fathers and their spouses and their descendants.) Those among them who are righteous will be joined with them, even if they did not reach the level of their virtue, following them and in magnification of their status, and so that they may be joyful with them, finding comfort in their company. Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): That He was asked about a believing man who has a believing wife, and they both enter Paradise: Will one of them marry the other? He said: "Indeed, Allah is a just Judge (حَكَمٌ عَدْلٌ). If he was superior to her, He will give him the choice, and if he chooses her, she will be among his wives. And if she was superior to him, He will give her the choice, and if she chooses him, he will be a husband to her." And in al-Khisal, narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): That Umm Salamah said to Him: "May my father and mother be sacrificed for you! A woman who has two husbands, and they both die and enter Paradise—to which of them will she belong?" He said: "O Umm Salamah, she will choose the one who was best in character (أَحْسَنَهُمَا خُلُقًا) and the best of them to his family. O Umm Salamah, indeed, good character (حُسْنُ الْخُلُقِ) has taken the goodness of this world and the Hereafter." **وَالْمَلائِكَةُ يَدْخُلُونَ عَلَيْهِمْ مِنْ كُلِّ بابٍ ** (and the angels will enter to them from every gate:) From the gates of their chambers and their palaces.

(24) سَلامٌ عَلَيْكُمْ بِما صَبَرْتُمْ ** (Peace be to you for your patience.) This is due to your patience. فَنِعْمَ عُقْبَى الدَّارِ.** (How excellent is the ultimate abode!)

Al-Qummi (may Allah have mercy upon him) said: It was revealed concerning the Imams (peace be upon them) and their followers (شيعتهم) who were patient. And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "We are patient (صُبَّرْ), but our followers (شيعتنا) are more patient than us, because we were patient with knowledge, while our followers were patient concerning that which they do not know."

In al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace), in a hadith describing the state of the believer when he enters the Gardens (الجنان) and the Chambers (الغرف). We shall mention the beginning of it in Surat Fatir and al-Zumar, if Allah wills. He said: Then Allah sends [to him/to him (variant reading)] a thousand angels who congratulate him on Paradise and marry him to the Hura'. They reach the first gate of His Gardens, and they say to the angel entrusted with the gates of the Gardens: "Seek permission for us to enter upon the friend of Allah (وليّ الله), for Allah has sent us as congratulators." The angel says: "I must first speak to the chamberlain (الحاجب) so that he may inform him of your location." He said: The angel enters to the chamberlain, and between him and the chamberlain are three Gardens, until he reaches the first gate. He says to the chamberlain: "Indeed, at the gate of the courtyard (العرصة) are a thousand angels sent by the Lord of the worlds (ربّ العالمين). They have come to congratulate the friend of Allah (وليّ الله), and they have asked me to seek permission for them to enter upon him." The chamberlain says to him: "It is difficult for me to seek permission for anyone to enter upon the friend of Allah while he is with his wife." He said: Between the chamberlain and the friend of Allah are two Gardens. The chamberlain enters upon the custodian (القيّم) and says to him: "Indeed, at the gate of the courtyard (العرصة) are a thousand angels sent by the Lord of the worlds (ربّ العالمين) who are congratulating the friend of Allah, so seek permission for them." The custodian (القيّم) rises to the servants (الخّدام) and says to them: "Indeed, the messengers of the Almighty (الجَّبار) are at the gate of the courtyard (العَرصة), and they are a thousand angels congratulating the friend of Allah, so inform him of their location." He said: They inform him. He said: Then permission is granted to them, and they enter upon the friend of Allah while He is in the Chamber (الغرفة), which has a thousand doors, and at every door is an angel entrusted with it. When permission is granted for the angels to enter upon the friend of Allah, every angel opens the door with which he was entrusted. The custodian (القيّم) then brings every angel through a door of the Chamber's doors, and they convey to him the message of the Almighty (الجبار). That is the saying of Allah, the Almighty: وَالْمَلائِكَةُ يَدْخُلُونَ عَلَيْهِمْ مِنْ كُلِّ بابٍ ** (and the angels enter upon them from every door) meaning, from the doors of the Chamber (الغرفة). سَلامٌ عَلَيْكُمْ بِما صَبَرْتُمْ فَنِعْمَ عُقْبَى الدَّارِ.** (Peace be to you for your patience. How excellent is the ultimate abode!)

(٢٥) وَالَّذِينَ يَنْقُضُونَ عَهْدَ اللهِ مِنْ بَعْدِ مِيثاقِهِ ** (25) But those who break Allah’s covenant after having pledged it [It means] after they confirmed it with affirmation (الإقرار) and acceptance (القبول). Al-Qummi said: It refers to the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين), and it is that [covenant] which Allah took from them in the primordial world (الذّرّ), and which the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم took from them at Ghadir Khumm. وَيَقْطَعُونَ ما أَمَرَ اللهُ بِهِ أَنْ يُوصَلَ ** and sever what Allah has commanded to be joined such as kinship (الرحم) and other things. وَيُفْسِدُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** and cause corruption in the earth through injustice (الظلم) and inciting discord (الفتن). أُولئِكَ لَهُمُ اللَّعْنَةُ وَلَهُمْ سُوءُ الدَّارِ ** for such there will be the curse, and for them will be the evil abode. [meaning] the punishment of the Fire (النّار).

(٢٦) اللهُ وحده يَبْسُطُ الرِّزْقَ لِمَنْ يَشاءُ وَيَقْدِرُ يوسّعه ويضيقه دون غيره وَفَرِحُوا بِالْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا بما بسط لهم فيها (26) Allah He alone expands the provision for whomever He wills and restricts it (يوسّعه ويضيقه)—He expands it and restricts it—without anyone else. And they rejoiced in the life of the world with what He expanded for them therein.

**وَمَا الْحَياةُ الدُّنْيا فِي الْآخِرَةِ **في جنب الآخرة **إِلَّا مَتاعٌ **إلا شيء قليل يتمتّع ثمّ يفنى ولا يدوم كعجالة الراكب يعني أنّهم اشروا (١) بما نالوا من الدنيا ولم يصرفوه فيما يستوجبون به نعيم الآخرة واغترّوا بما هو في جنبه نزر قليل النفع سريع الزّوال.
**and the life of the world in the Hereafter** compared to the Hereafter **is nothing but a temporary enjoyment** except a small thing that is enjoyed, then it perishes and does not last, like the brief provision (*كعجالة*) of the rider. Meaning that they became insolent (*اشروا*) (1) with what they attained from the world and did not spend it on what would make them deserve the bliss of the Hereafter, and they were deceived by what is, compared to it, a trifle (*نزر*), of little benefit, and quick to vanish.

(٢٧) وَيَقُولُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَوْ لا أُنْزِلَ عَلَيْهِ آيَةٌ مِنْ رَبِّهِ قُلْ إِنَّ اللهَ يُضِلُّ مَنْ يَشاءُ ** (27) The faithless say, 'Why has not a sign been sent down to him from his Lord?' Say, 'Indeed, Allah leads astray whomever He wishes, (They say this) by proposing further signs after the manifestation of the miracles. وَيَهْدِي إِلَيْهِ مَنْ أَنابَ ** and guides to Himself whomever turns penitently [to Him]. (It refers to) whoever accepted the truth and retreated from obstinacy.

(٢٨) **الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَتَطْمَئِنُّ قُلُوبُهُمْ بِذِكْرِ اللهِ ** (28) those who have faith and whose hearts find rest in the remembrance of Allah. Their hearts find rest in Him through intimacy with Him, reliance upon Him, and hope from Him.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They find rest through Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and He is the remembrance (ذكر) of Allah and His veil (حجابه).

And al-Qummi (said): الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ** (those who have faith) refers to the Shi'a (الشيعة), and the remembrance (ذكر) of Allah refers to the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and the Imams (peace be upon them). أَلا بِذِكْرِ اللهِ تَطْمَئِنُّ الْقُلُوبُ.** (28) Look! The hearts find rest in the remembrance of Allah!

footnote (١) اشر (like فرح - to be joyful) means he is أشر, وأشْر, وأشَر (with the *fath* on the *shin* and it can be vocalized), and أشران, meaning joyful (مرح). The plural forms are أشرون, وأشرون, وأشرى, and أشارى.

(٢٩) الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ طُوبى لَهُمْ ** (29) The ones who have faith and do righteous deeds—blessedness is for them (It is derived) from al-tayyib (الطيب), a verbal noun (مصدر) like bushra (بشرى) and zulfa (زلفى). وَحُسْنُ مَآبٍ means a good return (مرجع). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Blessedness (طُوبى) is a tree in Paradise, the root of which is in the house of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. There is no believer except that there is a branch of it in his house. No desire for anything crosses his heart except that it is brought to him by that (branch). And if an earnest rider were to travel in its shade for a hundred years, he would not emerge from it. And if a crow were to fly from its base, it would not reach its top until it fell down due to old age (هرماً). Behold, therefore, desire this (reward). And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): The like of it. And in al-Ikmal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Blessedness (طُوبى) is for the one who adheres to our command (أمرنا) during the occultation (غيبة) of our Qa'im (قائمنا), and whose heart does not deviate after guidance. He was asked: "And what is Blessedness (طُوبى)?" He said: "A tree in Paradise, the root of which is in the house of Ali ibn Abi Talib (may the blessings of Allah be upon him). There is no believer except that there is a branch from its branches in his house. And that is the saying of Allah: blessedness is for them and a good return (طُوبى لَهُمْ وَحُسْنُ مَآبٍ)." The narratives concerning the interpretation of Blessedness (طُوبى) as the tree in Paradise, and mentioning the descriptions (أوصاف) of that tree, are numerous. They were narrated by al-Qummi and al-Ayyashi in al-Uyun, al-Khisal, al-Ihtijaj, and other works. And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him), from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: That He was asked about طُوبى. He said: "A tree whose root is in My house and whose branches are over the people of Paradise." Then He was asked about it another time, and He صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "In the house of Ali (peace be upon him)." He was questioned about this, and He said: "Indeed, My house and the house of Ali (peace be upon him) in Paradise are in one place."

(٣٠) كَذلِكَ ** (Thus) Like that sending (الإرسال). أَرْسَلْناكَ فِي أُمَّةٍ قَدْ خَلَتْ مِنْ قَبْلِها ** (We sent you among a nation before which nations have passed away) Preceded it. أُمَمٌ ** (nations) Messengers were sent to them, so your sending to this nation is not unprecedented (ببدع). لِتَتْلُوَا عَلَيْهِمُ الَّذِي أَوْحَيْنا إِلَيْكَ ** (that you may recite to them what We have revealed to you) So that you may read to them the Book which We revealed to you. وَهُمْ يَكْفُرُونَ بِالرَّحْمنِ ** (while they disbelieve in the All-beneficent) And their state is that they disbelieve in the One of Vast Mercy (الواسع الرحمة) Whose favor (نعمته) has encompassed them and Whose mercy has encompassed everything, yet they did not thank His favors, especially the sending of someone like you to them and the sending down of this miraculous Quran (القرآن المعجز) upon them. قُلْ هُوَ رَبِّي ** (Say, 'He is my Lord!) Meaning, the All-beneficent is my Creator (خالقي) and the Manager of my affair (متوليّ أمري). لا إِلهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ** (There is no god except Him.) None deserves worship (العبادة) except Him. He is exalted above partners (الشركاء). عَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ ** (In Him I have put my trust) In my victory over you. وَإِلَيْهِ مَتابِ ** (and to Him is my return.') My return (مرجعي), so He will reward me for my perseverance (مصابرتكم) and struggle (مجاهدتكم) against you.

(٣١) وَلَوْ أَنَّ قُرْآناً سُيِّرَتْ بِهِ الْجِبالُ (31) If there were a Qur’ān whereby the mountains could be moved [meaning] they were shaken from their resting places (مقارها) أَوْ قُطِّعَتْ بِهِ الْأَرْضُ or the earth could be cut into pieces [meaning] it cracked from the fear of Allah and split open أَوْ كُلِّمَ بِهِ الْمَوْتى or the dead could be spoken to, so they would hear and respond, [then] it would have been this Qur’ān, due to the greatness of its worth and the majesty of its status. Al-Qummi said: If anything of the Qur’ān were like that, it would be this [Qur’ān]. And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): "And we have inherited this Qur’ān, in which is that by which the mountains are moved, the lands are cut into pieces, and the dead are brought to life." بَلْ لِلَّهِ الْأَمْرُ جَمِيعاً rather all command belongs to Allah. Rather, to Allah belongs the power over everything. أَفَلَمْ يَيْأَسِ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا Have not the faithful known It was said: meaning, "Have they not known (أفلم يعلم)," and this is the dialect of a people from the tribe of al-Nakhaʿ (النّخع). It was also said that al-ya’s (اليأس) [despair] was used in the meaning of knowledge (العلم) because it encompasses its meaning, for the one who despairs of a thing is certain (عالم) that it will not occur. And in al-Majmaʿ: ʿAli, ʿAli ibn al-Husayn, and Jaʿfar ibn Muhammad (peace be upon them) read [it as]: أفلم يتبين (Have they not become clear/manifest). It was said that this reading is attributed to a group of the Companions (الصّحابة) and the Successors (التابعين), and it is its interpretation (تفسيره). أَنْ لَوْ يَشاءُ اللهُ لَهَدَى النَّاسَ جَمِيعاً وَلا يَزالُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا تُصِيبُهُمْ بِما صَنَعُوا that had Allah wished, He would have guided all mankind? And disaster will not cease to befall the faithless because of what they have perpetrated, [meaning] from disbelief (الكفر) and evil deeds (سوءِ الأفعال) قارِعَةٌ (١) or alight near their homes, a calamity (داهية) that strikes (تقرعهم) them, consisting of various types of misfortunes affecting their souls and their wealth أَوْ تَحُلُ or alight the calamity (القارعة) قَرِيباً مِنْ دارِهِمْ (near their homes), so they are terrified by it and its sparks fly towards them, like the detachments (السرّايا) that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم sends out, which change their circumstances and snatch away their livestock. حَتَّى يَأْتِيَ وَعْدُ اللهِ إِنَّ اللهَ لا يُخْلِفُ الْمِيعادَ. until Allah’s promise comes. Indeed, Allah does not break His promise.

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding: وَلا يَزالُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا تُصِيبُهُمْ بِما صَنَعُوا قارِعَةٌ (And disaster will not cease to befall the faithless because of what they have perpetrated). He said: It is the retribution (النقمة). أَوْ تَحُلُّ قَرِيباً مِنْ دارِهِمْ (or alight near their homes), meaning: it alights upon a people other than them, so they see that and hear about it, and those upon whom it alighted are disobedient disbelievers (عصاة كفّار) like them, yet they do not take heed from one another. And they will not cease to be like that حَتَّى يَأْتِيَ وَعْدُ اللهِ (until Allah’s promise comes), which He promised the believers of victory (النّصر), and Allah disgraces the disbelievers (الكافِرينَ).

(١) Al-Qāriʿah (القارعة) is the affliction (البليّة) that strikes (تقرع) the heart due to the intensity of fear (المخافة). Al-Qarʿ (القرع) is striking with severe force (بشدّة الاعتماد). And the Qawāriʿ (قوارع) of time (الدّهر) are its calamities (دواهيه).

(٣٢) **وَلَقَدِ اسْتُهْزِئَ بِرُسُلٍ مِنْ قَبْلِكَ فَأَمْلَيْتُ لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ثُمَّ أَخَذْتُهُمْ ** (32) Certainly apostles have been ridiculed before you. But I gave the faithless a respite, then I seized them.

[This is] a consolation (تسلية) for the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and a threat (وعيد) to those who ridicule Him and those who propose demands upon Him. And al-imlāʾ (الاملاء) is that He leaves a period (ملأة) of time in security (أمن) and tranquility (ودعة).

And al-Qummi [said]: meaning, I prolonged hope (الأمل) for them, then I destroyed them.

**فَكَيْفَ كانَ عِقابِ ** (then how was My retribution?)

[Meaning] My retribution (عقابي) upon them.

(٣٣) أَفَمَنْ هُوَ قائِمٌ عَلى كُلِّ نَفْسٍ ** (33) Is He, Who is guardian over every soul, (He is) a watcher (رقيب) over it, a preserver (حافظ) بِما كَسَبَتْ ** with what it has earned, of good and evil. So nothing of their deeds is hidden from Him, nor does anything of their recompense escape Him, (is He) like one who is not so? وَجَعَلُوا لِلَّهِ شُرَكاءَ قُلْ سَمُّوهُمْ ** And they set up partners to Allah. Say, 'Name them! Who are they, or describe them. Then look, do they possess that by which they deserve worship (العبادة) and merit partnership (الشركة)? أَمْ تُنَبِّئُونَهُ ** Do you inform Him Rather, do you inform Him بِما لا يَعْلَمُ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** of something He does not know in the earth? with partners He does not know in the earth, while He is the Knower of what is in the heavens and the earth. For if He does not know them, then they are nothing to which knowledge relates. The intent is to negate that He has partners (شركاء). أَمْ بِظاهِرٍ مِنَ الْقَوْلِ ** Or is it by a superficial remark?' Or do you name them partners (شركاء) by a superficial remark (بِظاهِرٍ مِنَ الْقَوْلِ) without reality (حقيقة) or consideration (اعتبار), like naming the Ethiopian (الزّنجيّ) 'Camphor' (كافوراً)? And these styles (الأساليب) of argumentation (الاحتجاج) proclaim with an eloquent tongue that they are not the speech of humans (البشر). بَلْ زُيِّنَ لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مَكْرُهُمْ ** Rather, the plotting of the faithless has been made fair-seeming to them, their deception (تمويههم), so they imagined falsehoods (أباطيل) and then deemed them (true). وَصُدُّوا عَنِ السَّبِيلِ ** and they have been barred from the way. (meaning) the way of Truth (سبيل الحق). And it was read with the opening of the ṣād (بفتح الصاد) [i.e., صَدُّوا]. وَمَنْ يُضْلِلِ اللهُ ** Whomever Allah leads astray, (meaning) He forsakes him, فَما لَهُ مِنْ هادٍ ** there is no guide for him. who grants him success (يوفقه) for guidance (الهدى).

(٣٤) لَهُمْ عَذابٌ فِي الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا ** by killing, captivity (الأسر), and all other calamities (المصائب). وَلَعَذابُ الْآخِرَةِ أَشَقُ due to its severity and its permanence (ودوامه). وَما لَهُمْ مِنَ اللهِ مِنْ واقٍ meaning, no repeller (دافع). لَهُمْ عَذابٌ فِي الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا وَلَعَذابُ الْآخِرَةِ أَشَقُّ وَما لَهُمْ مِنَ اللَّهِ مِنْ واقٍ** (1) There is a punishment for them in the life of the world, and the punishment of the Hereafter is surely harder, and they have no defender against Allah.

(٣٥) مَثَلُ الْجَنَّةِ الَّتِي وُعِدَ الْمُتَّقُونَ ** meaning, its description (صفتها) which is a parable (مثل) in its wondrousness (الغرابة). تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ أُكُلُها دائِمٌ neither cut off (مقطوعة) nor forbidden (ممنوعة). وَظِلُّها ** likewise. تِلْكَ عُقْبَى الَّذِينَ اتَّقَوْا وَعُقْبَى الْكافِرِينَ النَّارُ. مَثَلُ الْجَنَّةِ الَّتِي وُعِدَ الْمُتَّقُونَ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ أُكُلُها دائِمٌ وَظِلُّها تِلْكَ عُقْبَى الَّذِينَ اتَّقَوْا وَعُقْبَى الْكافِرِينَ النَّارُ (2) The parable of the Garden promised to the Godwary: streams run in its lower reaches, its produce is perpetual, and its shade. That is the requital of those who are Godwary, and the requital of the faithless is the Fire.

(٣٦) وَالَّذِينَ آتَيْناهُمُ الْكِتابَ يَفْرَحُونَ بِما أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْكَ. (36) Those whom We have given the Book rejoice at what has been sent down to you.

القمّيّ narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Meaning, يَفْرَحُونَ ** (they rejoice) at the Book of Allah when it is recited to them, and when they recite it, their eyes overflow with tears out of fear (الفزع) and sorrow (الحُزن). مِنَ الْأَحْزابِ ** (from the factions) and from those who formed factions (تحزّب) against the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم with enmity (بالعداوة), مَنْ يُنْكِرُ بَعْضَهُ ** (some deny part of it), which is that which contradicts their laws (شرايعهم). قُلْ إِنَّما أُمِرْتُ أَنْ أَعْبُدَ اللهَ وَلا أُشْرِكَ بِهِ ** (Say: 'I have only been commanded to worship Allah and not to ascribe any partners to Him'). Thus, your denial (إنكاركم) is a denial of the worship of Allah and His Oneness (توحيده). إِلَيْهِ أَدْعُوا ** (To Him I invite), not to other than Him. وَإِلَيْهِ مَآبِ ** (and to Him is my return), and to Him is my place of return (مرجعي), not to other than Him. It was said: This means that this (principle) is what is agreed upon among the Prophets (الأنبياء). As for what is besides that, concerning the subsidiary matters (التفاريع), it is what differs according to the ages (العصور) and nations (الأمم). Thus, there is no meaning to your denial of the disagreement therein, while you yourselves say the like of that.

(٣٧) وَكَذلِكَ أَنْزَلْناهُ ** (37) And thus We have sent it down And like this sending down, We have sent it down, commanded within it with the worship of Allah, His Oneness (توحيده), and the call (الدّعوة) to Him and to His religion. حُكْماً عَرَبِيًّا ** a judgment in Arabic. An Arabic wisdom (حكمة عربية) translated in the language of the Arabs (بلسان العرب). وَلَئِنِ اتَّبَعْتَ أَهْواءَهُمْ ** And if you follow their desires In matters concerning which they call you to agree with them, after the knowledge has come to you regarding the abrogation (بنسخ ذلك) of that [matter], ما لَكَ مِنَ اللهِ مِنْ وَلِيٍ ** you will not have any guardian against Allah who will help you (ينصرك). وَلا واقٍ ** nor any protector. who will prevent the punishment (العقاب) from you. And this is a decisive termination (حسم) of their ambitions (أطماعهم) and an incitement (تهييج) for the believers to remain steadfast in their religion.

(٣٨) وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنا رُسُلاً مِنْ قَبْلِكَ ** (38) And certainly We sent apostles before you [They were] humans like you. وَجَعَلْنا لَهُمْ أَزْواجاً وَذُرِّيَّةً ** and We appointed for them wives and descendants [Meaning] wives and children, just as they are for you. In al-Jawami', [it is mentioned that] they used to reproach the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم for his frequent marrying of women. So, it was said that the Messengers before him were like him, possessing wives and descendants (ذوي أزواج وذرّيّة). Al-Ayyashi narrates from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was none other than one of those [Messengers]. Allah appointed for him wives and appointed for him descendants. None of those who embraced Islam with any of the Prophets were like those of his Household (أهل بيته) who embraced Islam with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. Allah honored His Messenger with this." And in another narration: "So, we are the descendants (ذرّية) of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم." وَما كانَ لِرَسُولٍ ** And it is not for any apostle And it was not right for him, nor was it within his capacity, أَنْ يَأْتِيَ بِآيَةٍ ** to bring a sign which is proposed to him or a decree (حكم) which is sought from him, إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ اللهِ except by Allah’s leave. For He is the One capable of that. لِكُلِّ أَجَلٍ كِتابٌ ** For every term there is a written decree. For every time there is a decree (حكم) written concerning the servants, and they receive what their welfare (صلاحهم) necessitates.

(39) يَمْحُوا اللهُ ما يَشاءُ وَيُثْبِتُ (Allah effaces whatever He wishes and confirms [whatever He wishes]) And it was recited with tashdid (وَيُثَبِّتُ). وَعِنْدَهُ أُمُّ الْكِتابِ (and with Him is the Mother of the Book.) Meaning the origin (أصل) of the Books, which is al-Lawh al-Mahfuz (اللوح المحفوظ - the Preserved Tablet), protected from effacement (المحو) and alteration (التبديل). It is comprehensive of everything, so in it is the confirmation (اثبات) of what is confirmed, the confirmation of what is effaced, its effacement, and the confirmation of its substitute. He abrogates (ينسخ) what ought to be abrogated and confirms what His wisdom necessitates. He effaces the sins (سيّئات) of the repentant and confirms good deeds (الحَسنات) in their place. And He effaces from the book of the guardian angels (كتاب الحفظة) what is not related to recompense (جزاء) and leaves other things confirmed. Or, He confirms what He sees in the core of His servant's heart, and effaces the corrupt things (الفاسدات) and confirms the existent things (الكائنات). He effaces one generation (قرناً) and confirms others. And the last meaning is narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), recorded in al-Majma', and it is one of its meanings, all of which are intended by it. He said: And this is like His saying, the Almighty: ثُمَّ أَنْشَأْنا مِنْ بَعْدِهِمْ قَرْناً آخَرِينَ (Then We brought forth other generations after them) and His saying: كَمْ أَهْلَكْنا قَبْلَهُمْ مِنَ الْقُرُون (How many generations We destroyed before them).

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Is anything effaced except what was confirmed? And is anything confirmed except what was not [previously confirmed]?

In al-Qummi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): When Laylat al-Qadr (ليلة القدر) occurs, the angels, the Spirit (الرّوح), and the scribes (الكتبة) descend to the lowest heaven and write down what will occur of Allah's decree (قضاء) for that year. Then, if Allah wills to advance something, or delay it, or decrease something, He commands the angel to efface what He wills, and then confirms what He intended.

And in al-Kafi: [A narration] with the same meaning.

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah presented to Adam the names of the Prophets and their lifespans—the hadith (الحديث). This has already passed in the latter part of Surat al-Baqarah, cited from al-Ilal (العِلل).

And from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about the saying of Allah, the Almighty: ادْخُلُوا الْأَرْضَ الْمُقَدَّسَةَ الَّتِي كَتَبَ اللهُ لَكُمْ (Enter the holy land which Allah has ordained for you). He said: He ordained it for them, then He effaced it, then He ordained it for their children, so they entered it. And يَمْحُوا اللهُ ما يَشاءُ وَيُثْبِتُ وَعِنْدَهُ أُمُّ الْكِتابِ. (Allah effaces whatever He wishes and confirms [whatever He wishes], and with Him is the Mother of the Book.)

And from him, narrated from his father (peace be upon them both), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Indeed, a man maintains his kinship (يصل رحمه) while only three years remain of his life, so Allah extends it for him to thirty-three years. And indeed, a man severs his kinship (يقطع رحمه) while thirty-three years remain of his life, so Allah decreases it for him to three years or less." He said: And Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) used to recite this Ayah.

And from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about the saying of Allah, the Almighty: يَمْحُوا اللهُ ما يَشاءُ وَيُثْبِتُ وَعِنْدَهُ أُمُّ الْكِتابِ (Allah effaces whatever He wishes and confirms [whatever He wishes], and with Him is the Mother of the Book). He said: "Indeed, that Book is the book in which يَمْحُوا اللهُ (Allah effaces) whatever He wishes and confirms [whatever He wishes]. Among that is the supplication (الدعاء) that repels the decree (القضاء). And that supplication is written upon it, by which the decree is repelled. However, when it reaches أُمُّ الْكِتابِ (the Mother of the Book), the supplication is of no avail therein."

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: They are two books: a book besides Umm al-Kitab (أُمُّ الْكِتابِ), from which Allah effaces ما يَشاءُ وَيُثْبِتُ (whatever He wishes and confirms [whatever He wishes]), and أُمُّ الْكِتابِ (the Mother of the Book), from which nothing is changed. And from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They are two matters: the suspended (موقوف) and the determined (محتوم). Whatever is determined, He executes it. And whatever is suspended, He has the Will (المشيَّة) concerning it; He decrees concerning it whatever He wills.

And al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), that he said: Ali ibn al-Husayn (peace be upon them both) used to say: "Were it not for an Ayah in the Book of Allah, I would inform you of what will happen until the Day of Resurrection." I asked him: "Which Ayah?" He said: The saying of Allah: يَمْحُوا اللهُ ما يَشاءُ وَيُثْبِتُ وَعِنْدَهُ أُمُّ الْكِتابِ (Allah effaces whatever He wishes and confirms [whatever He wishes], and with Him is the Mother of the Book). And a similar narration is found in al-Tawhid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him).

And in al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Knowledge (العلم) (1) is two kinds: Knowledge stored with Allah, which He has not disclosed to any of His creation; and Knowledge which He taught to His angels and His messengers. As for what He taught to His angels and His messengers, it shall certainly come to pass, and He does not belie Himself, nor His angels, nor His messengers. And [the other] Knowledge stored with Him, He advances from it what He wills, and delays what He wills, and confirms what He wills.

I say: And perhaps some of His messengers rarely know from His stored Knowledge, as the narrations (الأخبار) have stated. By this, reconciliation is achieved between this hadith (الحديث) and the one preceding it. The complete verification (تحقيق) of this subject is to be sought in our book called al-Wafi (الوافي), in the chapters concerning the knowledge of Allah's creatures and His actions, from the first volume thereof.

(1) A clarification (بيان): This is because the form (صورة) of all existent things (الكائنات) is inscribed in أُمُّ الْكِتابِ (the Mother of the Book), sometimes called al-Lawh al-Mahfuz (اللوح المحفوظ), which is the Intellectual World (العالم العقليّ) and the First Creation (الخلق الأوّل). And [it is inscribed] in the Book of Effacement and Confirmation (كتاب المحو والإثبات) at other times, which is the Psychic World (العالم النّفسي) and the Second Creation (الخلق الثاني). The Prophets and Messengers are mostly informed about the First [Book], which is preserved from effacement and confirmation, and its ruling is determined (محتوم), unlike the Second [Book], which is suspended (موقوف). In the First [Book] is the confirmation of effacement in the Second [Book], and the confirmation of confirmation therein, and the effacement of confirmation when the decree occurs and another matter is initiated. Thus, it is sanctified (مقدّس) from effacement; it decrees the variation of matters and their detailed outcomes, inscribed by the determination (تقدير) of the All-mighty, the All-knowing. (Wafi - وافي).

(٤٠) وَإِنْ ما نُرِيَنَّكَ بَعْضَ الَّذِي نَعِدُهُمْ أَوْ نَتَوَفَّيَنَّكَ ** (40) Whether We show you some of what We promise them, or take you away [before that] And however the situation turns out, We will show you some of what We promised them, or We will take you away before it. فَإِنَّما عَلَيْكَ الْبَلاغُ ** (your duty is only to communicate) nothing else. **وَعَلَيْنَا الْحِسابُ ** (and the reckoning is upon Us) for the recompense, not upon you. So, do not concern yourself with their turning away (1), nor hasten their punishment. We are certainly going to do it, and this is its vanguard (2).

(٤١) **أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا أَنَّا نَأْتِي الْأَرْضَ نَنْقُصُها مِنْ أَطْرافِها ** (41) Do they not see that We visit the land, reducing it from its edges?

(It is) by the departure of its inhabitants. In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He means by that what perishes of the generations (القرون), and He named it a visitation (اتياناً).

And in al-Faqih, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, and He said: The loss of the scholars (العلماء).

And al-Qummi said: The death of its scholars (علمائها). And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Ali ibn al-Husayn (peace be upon them both) used to say: What comforts my soul (يسخي نفسي) regarding the swiftness of death and killing among us is the saying of Allah, the Almighty: أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا أَنَّا نَأْتِي الْأَرْضَ نَنْقُصُها مِنْ أَطْرافِها ** (Do they not see that We visit the land, reducing it from its edges?) and that is the departure of the scholars (العلماء).

I say: Based on this interpretation (التفسير), al-atraf (الأطراف) would be the plural of taraf (طرف) (3) or taraf (طرف) with taskin (vowelless middle letter), meaning the scholars (العلماء) and the nobles (الأشراف), as mentioned in al-Gharbiyyin.

**وَاللهُ يَحْكُمُ لا مُعَقِّبَ لِحُكْمِهِ ** (41) Allah judges, and there is none to reverse His judgment.

There is no repeller (رادّ) for it. And al-mu'aqqib (المعقب) is the one who follows a thing and nullifies it (فيبطله).

**وَهُوَ سَرِيعُ الْحِسابِ ** (41) and He is swift at reckoning.

So He will reckon with them shortly.

(٤٢) وَقَدْ مَكَرَ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ (42) Those who were before them also plotted. against their prophets and the believers among them فَلِلَّهِ الْمَكْرُ جَمِيعاً But all plotting belongs to Allah. since no plot besides His plot is regarded, for He is the One capable of achieving what is intended by it, unlike others. Al-Qummi said: Plotting (المكر) from Allah is the punishment (العذاب). يَعْلَمُ ما تَكْسِبُ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ He knows what every soul earns. so He prepares its recompense, and it comes to it from where they do not perceive. وَسَيَعْلَمُ الْكُفَّارُ (and it was read al-Kāfir) لِمَنْ عُقْبَى الدَّارِ Soon the faithless will know for whom is the ultimate outcome of the [final] abode. from the two parties (meaning the praiseworthy outcome). And this is like an explanation for Allah's plotting against them.

(٤٣) And the faithless say, 'You are not an apostle.' Say, 'Allah is sufficient as a witness between me and you,' for He has manifested such proofs (الحجج) of My apostleship that they suffice without a witness testifying to it, 'and he who possesses the knowledge of the Book.' In al-Kafi, al-Khara'ij, and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He meant us, and Ali is the first of us, the most excellent of us, and the best of us after the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The like of it. In al-Ihtijaj: A man asked Ali ibn Abi Talib (may the blessings of Allah be upon him) about the most excellent virtue (منقبة) belonging to him, so he recited the verse and said: He meant me by 'and he who possesses the knowledge of the Book.' In al-Majalis, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: He was asked about this verse, and He said: That is my brother, Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him). And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It was said to him: This is the son of Abdullah ibn Salam, who claims that his father is the one whom Allah refers to when He says: 'Say, 'Allah is sufficient as a witness between me and you, and he who possesses the knowledge of the Book.' He (peace be upon him) said: He lied. It is Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him). And narrated from him (peace be upon him): It was revealed concerning Ali (peace be upon him), that he is the scholar (عالم) of this Ummah after the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. And in al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He is the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). And he was asked whether the one 'who possessed knowledge of the Book' (الَّذِي عِنْدَهُ عِلْمٌ مِنَ الْكِتابِ) was more knowledgeable, or the one 'who possesses the knowledge of the Book' (عِنْدَهُ عِلْمُ الْكِتابِ). He replied: What the one 'who possessed knowledge of the Book' (الَّذِي عِنْدَهُ عِلْمٌ مِنَ الْكِتابِ) had, compared to the one 'who possesses the knowledge of the Book' (عِنْدَهُ عِلْمُ الْكِتابِ), is only the amount a mosquito takes with its wing from the water of the sea. And the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said: Indeed, the knowledge that Adam brought down from heaven to earth, and all that by which the Prophets were favored up to the Seal of the Prophets, is in the progeny (عترة) of the Seal of the Prophets. In al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Did you find in what you read in the Book of Allah, the Almighty: Said he who possessed knowledge of the Book, 'I will bring it to you before your glance returns to you.' Then he mentioned what is close to what was mentioned, but in a more elaborate manner, and said at the end of it: The knowledge of the Book (عِلْمُ الْكِتاب), by Allah, all of it is with us. The knowledge of the Book (عِلْمُ الْكِتاب), by Allah, all of it is with us. In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (may the blessings of Allah be upon him): Whoever frequently recites Surat al-Ra'd (سورة الرّعد), Allah will never afflict him with a thunderbolt, even if he were a Nasibi (ناصبيّاً). And if he is a believer (مؤمناً), he will enter Paradise without reckoning and will intercede for all those He knows from his household (أهل بيته) and his brothers.

Surat Ibrahim (peace be upon him)

It is Meccan, except for two verses which were revealed concerning the polytheists killed at Badr: (Have you not regarded those who changed Allah's blessing) up to His saying (and evil is the abode.) The number of its verses is fifty-five verses. In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful
